title: Behavioral Evidence, Values, Equity, and Accessible Hiring · Career Decision Retrieval
summary: A delayed hiring review reconstructs behavioral evidence, values, equity, and accessible hiring from target, claim, evidence, audience, action, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, consent, owner, and next-decision evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for behavioral evidence and access response bank after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose a specific relevant situation and explain context, responsibility, action, decision, observable result, reflection, and transfer limit.
Describe disagreement, feedback, error, repair, shared credit, power, and changed behavior without blame or false harmony.
Test stated organizational values with recent behavior, incentives, tradeoffs, failure response, affected people, and employee recourse.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Behavior Example
steps.instruction:
Order the Behavior Example evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Behavior Example.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Behavior Example assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Behavior Example, keep Behavior Example connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: Memorized heroic stories erase collaborators, blame a former team, treat culture fit as objective, and presume every assessment is equally accessible. The review variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Choose a specific relevant situation and explain context, responsibility, action, decision, observable result, reflection, and transfer limit.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Behavior Example has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Behavior Example, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Behavior Example precondition, or invariant that must hold.

The review variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Choose a specific relevant situation and explain context, responsibility, action, decision, observable result, reflection, and transfer limit.

For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Behavior Example, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: A polished hypothetical answer is stronger than an imperfect real example.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints for the review Behavior Example case
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure for the review Behavior Example case

stimulus.title: Jurisdiction Boundary case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Memorized heroic stories erase collaborators, blame a former team, treat culture fit as objective, and presume every assessment is equally accessible. The review variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Distinguish general ethical practice from location-specific rights, protected categories, disclosure rules, background checks, and qualified advice.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: U.S. employment guidance applies globally and permanently.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish general ethical practice from location-specific rights, protected categories, disclosure rules, background checks, and qualified advice. Artifact under review: behavioral evidence and access response bank.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Jurisdiction Boundary, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Jurisdiction Boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Jurisdiction Boundary
steps.instruction:

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish general ethical practice from location-specific rights, protected categories, disclosure rules, background checks, and qualified advice.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Behavior Example to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Behavior Example.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using general and ethical plus specific case evidence.
